mechanism_narrative: Sustained strength in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. This signal is always active and does not depend on thresholds.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year rank IC of RSAFS monthly change vs 20-day forward return is below 0.05, or any 12-month period with positive RSAFS trend yields negative average SPX return.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, the labor market becomes extremely tight, signaling late-cycle wage pressures and potential profit margin compression. This triggers a negative forward return expectation over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical tightening cycles often preceded equity corrections.
failure_condition: In > 40% of instances when UNRATE < 4.0%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of low policy rates (FEDFUNDS ≤ 2.5%) and rising industrial production (INDPRO > 105) reflects an accommodative monetary stance combined with real economic momentum. This interaction drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings growth is supported by favorable credit conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates and strong production historically coincide with bull markets.
failure_condition: When the joint condition holds, the median 60-day forward SPX return is ≤ 0, or the hit rate of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units signal strong residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to GDP growth and positive equity returns over the following month. The threshold identifies the upper end of housing activity that historically precedes expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a leading economic indicator with multiplier effects.
failure_condition: In > 50% of months with HOUST > 1500, the forward 20-day SPX return is negative, or the average return is not statistically positive at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profits and economic output, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The threshold filters for genuine downturns.
evidence_refs: fact:Manufacturing recessions often lead broader economic downturns.
failure_condition: When the trigger activates, the average 20-day SPX return is positive, or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55% over all triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of low unemployment (UNRATE ≤ 4.5%) and low inflation (CPIAUCSL ≤ 2.5% YoY) defines a Goldilocks macroeconomic regime where strong labor markets coexist with price stability. This interaction supports positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings grow without overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:Goldilocks periods in the late 1990s and mid-2010s preceded strong equity gains.
failure_condition: The average 20-day SPX return during Goldilocks periods is negative or not significantly positive, or the hit rate drops below 60% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment gains exceeding 200,000 signal strong labor demand and income growth, boosting consumer spending and corporate revenues. This threshold-triggered signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon as markets react to payroll strength.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong payrolls are closely watched by market participants.
failure_condition: Over a rolling 3-year window, the 5-day forward return following strong payroll prints has a median ≤ 0, or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: GDP growth, measured by the initial point-in-time estimate despite high revision risk, provides a fundamental anchor for equity valuations. Rising real GDP supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon regardless of the business cycle phase.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic output and is positively correlated with corporate profits.
failure_condition: The quarterly rank IC of GDPC1 growth versus subsequent 60-day SPX return is below 0.05 over any 5-year window, or consecutive quarters of positive GDP are followed by negative equity returns more than 40% of the time.
